summary: Phase 3 testing: 8 NEW backend tests added covering share links (3), saved items (1), booking prices (1), conversational edit (3). Full pytest suite now 23/23 passing. Frontend sanity verified for /share/<bogus>, /saved, /chat (desktop split-panel + mobile chat-only), /itinerary/trip-paris-001 (live prices visible: '$93 via GetYourGuide ★4.7 (975)'), and bookmark click flow (POST /api/saved + 'Saved' toast confirmed).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/trips/{id}/edit: LLM call has 90s timeout per provider with gemini→claude fallback. Edit prompt truncates trip JSON to 14000 chars (line 535) — acceptable for current schema sizes but could clip very long trips silently. Worth adding a warning log if truncation happens.

server.py /api/saved POST: silently allows guest_session_id to be ignored when user is signed in (correct precedence) but does not validate that activity_id/trip_id belong to caller — fine for MVP, low risk since saved items are scoped per-user/guest on read.
server.py /api/booking/prices: deterministic mock with 0.4s artificial sleep. Fine for demo. Consider caching the price dict in memory per id since recompute is tiny — minor.
server.py /api/share/{token}: token is uuid4 hex[:18] (~72 bits) — adequate entropy for unguessability. No expiry on shares — by design.

Saved.jsx falls back to mock SAVED_ITEMS when the live list is empty (line 22). This gives a nicer first-touch UX but means the empty-state component (EmptySaved) is unreachable for fresh users — intentional product choice, just worth noting.

ActivityCard.jsx bookmark POSTs to /api/saved on every click without checking duplicates — clicking the same bookmark twice will create two saved-items rows (toggle behaviour is purely UI-local, not synced). Low-priority enhancement.
Itinerary.jsx makes 2 calls to /api/booking/prices on mount (React StrictMode in dev double-invokes effects). Harmless in prod but a useRef-guarded fetch would silence the duplicate.
Chat.jsx mobile-view toggle works correctly — itinerary panel hidden at <md breakpoint as designed.
All new endpoints respect ownership checks (user_id vs guest_session_id) consistently — verified via 403 tests for /share, /edit.
test_credentials: Auth tested via mongosh-seeded user_sessions row + Authorization: Bearer header (per /app/auth_testing.md). No real Google account used.
seed_data_creation: Phase 3 tests reuse the existing session-scoped generated_trip fixture (1 real Gemini 3 Pro generate call) and a session-scoped seeded_session fixture for authed flows. Saved-items test uses a unique guest_session_id per run. test_zz_delete_trip ensures generated trip is cleaned up at the end. Real LLM calls: 2 (generate + edit), each <90s.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 is fully wired and verified. New endpoints: POST /api/trips/:id/share, GET /api/share/:token, POST /api/trips/:id/edit, GET/POST/DELETE /api/saved, GET /api/booking/prices. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_memento_api.py -v` (23 tests, ~100s, includes 2 real LLM calls). Frontend sanity confirmed: /share/<bogus> shows 'Share not found' + 'Plan your own' CTA; /saved fetches API and falls back to mock items when empty; /chat is split-panel on desktop and chat-only on mobile (<768px); /itinerary/trip-paris-001 renders live booking prices ('$93 via GetYourGuide ★4.7 (975)') after skeletons; bookmark click POSTs to /api/saved and shows 'Saved' toast. Note that /itinerary/trip-paris-001 uses bundled mock trip data — share/edit operations there will 404 (by design, see other_misc_info). Mapbox console error 'Failed to execute postMessage' is benign and unrelated to Phase 3.
rca of the issue: No bugs. All Phase 3 acceptance criteria green.
